name: Trellis
description_short: Arizona-based non-profit mortgage banker offering homeownership counseling, down payment assistance, and affordable housing development for first-time homebuyers.
description_long:
Trellis is a non-profit organization based in Arizona dedicated to expanding homeownership access and building affordable housing communities. Founded as the only non-profit mortgage banker in Arizona, Trellis combines lending services with comprehensive counseling to support individuals and families working toward homeownership. The organization operates under NMLS 167455 and maintains mortgage banker licensing, demonstrating legitimate lending credentials.

Trellis offers a comprehensive suite of homeownership services including HUD-certified homebuyer education classes, individual pre-purchase counseling with homeownership advisors, a financial capability program combining financial literacy classes with individual coaching, and access to Arizona is Home (AIH) down payment and closing cost assistance for first-time homebuyers. As a mortgage banker, they provide lending options directly to borrowers. Additionally, they engage in real estate development and community revitalization projects, including work along the McDowell Road Commercial Corridor in Phoenix.

What distinguishes Trellis is its dual mission combining both lending and community development. Unlike traditional mortgage lenders focused solely on loan origination, Trellis integrates financial education, pre-purchase counseling, and down payment assistance programs. Their non-profit structure and HUD-certified instructors signal alignment with homebuyer protection standards. The organization's involvement in real estate development and community building suggests a long-term commitment to systemic housing access rather than transactional lending.

A limitation noted in the website is the absence of specific financial metrics—years in business, number of homebuyers served, counseling client counts, and homes built are listed but not populated with actual figures. This lack of transparency about scale and impact makes it difficult to assess organizational reach. Prospective borrowers should inquire directly about lending terms, interest rates, and specific down payment assistance amounts, which are not disclosed on the website.
pros: Only non-profit mortgage banker in Arizona, combining lending with mission-driven services
HUD-certified homebuyer education instructors ensuring quality financial education
Down payment and closing cost assistance available through Arizona is Home program
Individual pre-purchase counseling with dedicated homeownership advisors
Financial capability program offering financial literacy classes and individual coaching
Real estate development division actively creating affordable housing units
Community involvement including revitalization projects on McDowell Road Commercial Corridor
cons: Website does not disclose specific mortgage terms, interest rates, or lending requirements
Impact metrics (years operating, homebuyers served, homes built) are not populated, limiting transparency
Limited geographic scope—services appear restricted to Arizona only
Down payment assistance details and eligibility requirements not specified on main website
No information provided about loan types, maximum loan amounts, or credit score requirements
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Arizona seeking counseling and down payment assistance
Borrowers prioritizing non-profit lending with financial education components
Low-to-moderate income families working toward homeownership in Phoenix area
Individuals interested in community-focused lending aligned with housing development
diagnosis: Trellis is best for Arizona-based first-time homebuyers seeking a non-profit mortgage lender that combines lending with comprehensive financial counseling and down payment assistance. Primary caveat: the website lacks specific lending terms, interest rates, and eligibility criteria, requiring direct contact with the organization to understand actual borrowing costs and loan options.
services: Mortgage lending through NMLS-licensed mortgage banker operations
Individual pre-purchase counseling with homeownership advisors
Financial capability program with financial literacy classes
Down payment assistance through Arizona is Home program
Closing cost assistance for first-time homebuyers
Real estate development and affordable housing creation
Community building and neighborhood revitalization initiatives
Homeownership goal assessment and personalized path planning
Community events and fundraisers supporting organizational programs
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
